title: The Government Educational and Training Institutions Ordinance, 1961 (East Pakistan Ordinance)
title_en: The Government Educational and Training Institutions Ordinance, 1961 (East Pakistan Ordinance)
description: An Ordinance to establish Boards of Governors for the Government Educational and Training Institutions in [Bangladesh]. WHEREAS it is expedient to establish Boards of Governors for some Government Educational and Training Institutions in [Bangladesh] for the efficient administration, control and management thereof; NOW, THEREFORE, in pursuance of the Presidential Proclamation of the seventh day of October, 1958, a...

provisions.title: 1. Short title, extent, commencement and application
provisions.content:
1. (1) This Ordinance may be called the [* * *] Government Educational and Training Institutions Ordinance, 1961.
(2) It extends to the whole of [Bangladesh].
(3) It shall come into force at once.
(4) It shall apply to such institutions and from such dates as may be notified by the [* * *] Government.

provisions.title: 2. Definitions
3. Establishment of a Board
provisions.content:
3. (1) The [* * *] Government may establish a Board of Governors for the efficient management and control of an institution.
(2) The Board shall be a body corporate and have perpetual succession and a common seal, with power to enter into contract and to acquire and hold property, movable and immovable, and shall, by its corporate name, sue and be sued.

provisions.title: 4. Constitution of the Board
provisions.content:
4. (1) The Board shall consist of not more than [fifteen] members to be appointed by [the President of Bangladesh]; and the number of official members and non official members shall be in such proportion as may be fixed by the [President].
(2) One of the members shall be appointed by [the President of Bangladesh] to be the Chairman of the Board.
(3) Official members, if any, shall be appointed by designation.
(4) Non official members, if any, shall hold office for a period of 3 years and shall be eligible for re appointment.
(5) The Principal shall be the Secretary of the Board.

provisions.title: 5. Removal of members
provisions.content: 5. [The President of Bangladesh] may remove any member before the expiry of his term of office without assigning any reason therefore.

provisions.title: 6. Meetings of the Board
provisions.content: 6. The meetings of the Board shall be held at such times, in such places and in accordance with such procedure as may be prescribed.

provisions.title: 7. General powers of the Board
8. Provisions regarding staff
provisions.content:
8. Any person serving, in connection with the affairs of the [State], in an institution in any capacity immediately before the date notified under sub section (4) of section 1, may be transferred by the [* * *] Government for service under the Board on such terms and conditions as the [* * *] Government may determine:
Provided that such terms and conditions shall not be less favourable than those which would have been admissible to him if he had not been so transferred; and no such person shall be dismissed, removed from service or reduced in rank by an authority subordinate to that by which he was appointed.

provisions.title: 9. Property
provisions.content: 9. All property, movable or immovable, vesting in the [* * *] Government in connection with the affairs of the institution immediately before the date notified under sub section (4) of section 1, shall vest in the Board with effect from the said date; and in the event of the [* * *] Government rescinding the notification made under sub section (4) of section 1, the property vesting in the Board shall stand transferred to the [* * *] Government.

provisions.title: 10. Board Fund
11. Maintenance of accounts
provisions.content: 11. The Board shall maintain its accounts in such form and manner as may be prescribed.

provisions.title: 12. Annual financial statements
provisions.content: 12. The Board shall submit a statement of estimated receipts and expenditures in respect of the next financial year to the [* * *] Government for approval at such time and in such manner as may be prescribed, and shall give effect to any modifications made therein by the [* * *] Government.

provisions.title: 13. Returns and Statements
provisions.content:
13. (1) As soon as may be, after the close of every financial year but not later than the last day of September next following, the Board shall submit to the [* * *] Government for approval a report on the conduct of the affairs of the Board for that year.
(2) The [* * *] Government may require the Board to furnish-
(a) any return, statement, estimate, statistics or other information or report regarding any matter under the administration, control or management of the Board, or
(b) a copy of any document in the custody of the Board, and the Board shall comply with every such requisition.

provisions.title: 14. Audit
provisions.content:
14. (1) The accounts of the Board shall be audited in such manner as may be prescribed.
(2) Copies of the Audit Report shall be sent to the Board and, with the comments of the Board, to the [* * *] Government.
(3) The Board shall carry out any directive issued by the [* * *] Government for rectification of an audit objection.

provisions.title: 15. Chairman, members and employees to be public servants
provisions.content: 15. The Chairman and members of the Board, the members of the Executive Committee and the Standing Committees constituted under this Ordinance, and the officers and employees of the institution and other officers and employees appointed under this Ordinance shall, when acting or purporting to act in pursuance of the provisions of this Ordinance, be deemed to be public servants within the meaning of section 21 of the [* * *] Penal Code, 1860 .

provisions.title: 16. Indemnity
provisions.content: 16. No suit, prosecution or legal proceeding shall lie against the Board, Chairman, members or officers and employees of the Board or institution in respect of anything done or intended to be done, in good faith, under this Ordinance.

provisions.title: 17. Power to make rules
18. Regulations
provisions.content: 18. The Board may, subject to the previous approval of the [* * *] Government, make regulations, not inconsistent with the provisions of this Ordinance and the rules made thereunder, to provide for all matters for which provision is necessary or expedient for carrying out the purposes of this Ordinance.

definitions.definition: the Board of Governors of an institution established under section 3

an educational or training institution set up, or to be set up, and notified by the [* * *] Government under sub-section (4) of section 1, and includes an establishment or organisation attached thereto
prescribed by rules made under this Ordinance
the principal officer in charge of an institution by whatever style he may be designated, and includes any officer for the time being performing his functions.
